zh.lifeSummary: 當Edmund Masterson於1544年出生在英國肯特阿福德時，他的父親Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns22歲，母親Joan Beeching26歲。他和Joane Beching至少有2個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1621年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加享年77歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼普萊姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Masterson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English maister ‘master’ + -son. Compare Master .
Irish: in Ireland, this is usually the English name in 1 above. However, it was also adopted for Irish Mac an Mháighistir ‘son of the master’, also Anglicized as McMaster .

variants.name: Master
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Amar, Dhimant, Pravin, Amit, Aruna, Bhanu, Gaurang, Gopal, Haren, Jagu, Jayesh, Kalpesh.

English: occupational name from Middle English maister ‘master’ (Old French maistre). The word had a wide range of applications, and the reference in surnames may be to a schoolmaster, to the master of a farm or house, to the master of an apprentice, or to a ship's captain. In Scotland the eldest sons of barons and the uncles of lords were called Masters, but this is not primarily a Scottish surname.
German: dialect form of   Meister   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: McMaster
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mháighistir ‘son of the master’, meaning ‘teacher’ or ‘cleric’. It could also be applied in the sense of ‘lord’ or ‘owner’. This name was sometimes Anglicized as Masterson .

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund Masterson was born in 1544, in Ashford, Kent, England, his father, Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns, was 22 and his mother, Joan Beeching, was 26. He had at least 2 sons and 5 daughters with Joane Beching. He died in 1621, in British Colonial America, at the age of 77, and was buried in Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund Masterson(이)가 1544년 영국 켄트 애스h퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Beeching(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Joane Beching(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1621년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 플림우스에 묻혔다.

ru.Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colon: плимоут, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edmund Masterson родился в 1544 г. в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Beeching, 26. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей от Joane Beching. Он умер в 1621 г. в местности бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности плимоут, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
